Name: BGEMIS0601
Sampling.area.Project: BGE Marine Invasive Species Citizen Science
Description: Sea Water; 20 by 50ml syringe pumps through eDNA filter;see comments
Sampling.event.Event.description: Burela harbour inside wall; The tip of the 50ml syringe broke off and got caught in the filter inlet- preventing the water from being sucked in properly. In order to avoid wasting the filter- the procedure was modified instead of suctioning we had to push the water into the filter (using the opposite imput)- so maintaining the correct flow direction as recommended in the protocol.Due to the clogging of the broken syringe tip- part of the buffer was lost. So I had to force the removal of the syringe tip to achieve a proper fit of the cap.  Please consider this replica as doubtful.... With the rest of the replicas there were no incidents.

Sampling.area.Project: BGE Marine Invasive Species Citizen Science
Description: Sea Water; 20 by 50ml syringe pumps through eDNA filter;see comments
Sampling.event.Event.description: Burela harbour inside wall; The tip of the 50ml syringe broke off and got caught in the filter inlet- preventing the water from being sucked in properly. In order to avoid wasting the filter- the procedure was modified instead of suctioning we had to push the water into the filter (using the opposite imput)- so maintaining the correct flow direction as recommended in the protocol.Due to the clogging of the broken syringe tip- part of the buffer was lost. So I had to force the removal of the syringe tip to achieve a proper fit of the cap.  Please consider this replica as doubtful.... With the rest of the replicas there were no incidents.

Description: Sea Water; 20 by 50ml syringe pumps through eDNA filter;see comments
Sampling.event.Event.description: Burela harbour inside wall; The tip of the 50ml syringe broke off and got caught in the filter inlet- preventing the water from being sucked in properly. In order to avoid wasting the filter- the procedure was modified instead of suctioning we had to push the water into the filter (using the opposite imput)- so maintaining the correct flow direction as recommended in the protocol.Due to the clogging of the broken syringe tip- part of the buffer was lost. So I had to force the removal of the syringe tip to achieve a proper fit of the cap.  Please consider this replica as doubtful.... With the rest of the replicas there were no incidents.
